title: update tools dependencies
body:
First fix the go mod errors and then bump gitvalidation.

@cevich PTAL

comments.body: Thanks!  Okay, once CI passes, we need to run `bors try` to verify it will pass branch-testing.  That's where the real trouble was.

```
[+0073s] make -C tests/tools
[+0073s] make[1]: Entering directory '/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/tools'
[+0073s] make[1]: Nothing to be done for 'all'.
[+0073s] make[1]: Leaving directory '/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/tools'
[+0074s] + exec git-validation -v -run DCO,short-subject -range 84410561bf96b64da4282872f11bb373d553163c..HEAD
[+0075s] time="2020-09-17T10:02:47-04:00" level=error msg="mm[git] cmd: \"git rev-list 84410561bf96b64da4282872f11bb373d553163c..HEAD\""
[+0075s] 2020/09/17 10:02:47 exit status 128
[+0075s] make: *** [Makefile:97: validate] Error 1
[10:02:47] END - [+0075s] total duration since START
```

From `bors try` says there's still some problem with `git-validate` :cry:

hmmm....well if I run manually on the `trying` branch using the same command, it passes.  Perhaps the `exit 128` (above) is due to the job being canceled from @rhatdan "bors r+" (just a guess).

Dan: Is there an alternative to git-validate?  I'm afraid we're starting to chase our own tails. It would be good to have a backup plan other than "disable git-validate" (assuming the actual checks are useful).
yep, as suspected, there's a new problem or the original one isn't 100% fixed.  Worse, it doesn't reproduce for me even when testing on the branch :confounded:
Hmmm...another idea: disable git-validation when running on branches (leave it running on PRs).  Though this really just kicks the can down the road.
